title: Compliance and Monitoring - Canada.ca
content:
## Compliance and Monitoring
The compliance and monitoring of controlled drugs and substances in Canada is overseen by Health Canada's Office of Controlled Substances , in conjunction with law enforcement agencies. The following pages provide general menus of all application forms, policy documents, and information pertinent to all stakeholders whose work involves controlled substances and precursor chemicals.
- Documents and Reports - Compliance and Monitoring of Controlled Substances
All legislative documents related to controlled substances can be found on the Department of Justice Canada website at the following addresses:
- Controlled Drugs and Substances Act
- Benzodiazepines and Other Targeted Substances Regulations
- Narcotic Control Regulations
- Precursor Control Regulations
- Industrial Hemp Regulations
- Marihuana for Medical Purposes Regulations
- Controlled Drugs and Substances Act (Police Enforcement) Regulations
- Qualification for Designations as Analysts Regulations
- Regulations Exempting Certain Precursors and Controlled Substances from the Application of the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act
- New Class of Practioners Regulations
- Parts G and J of the Food and Drug Regulations
International conventions and related articles and schedules can be found on the International Narcotics Control Board (INCB) website.
